title: Pabstiella tenera
taxonomy: Pabstiella tenera is a species of orchid in the genus Pabstiella, which is part of the family Orchidaceae. It is a terrestrial orchid that grows in the wild, typically found in tropical and subtropical regions. The classification of Pabstiella tenera includes its placement in the tribe Pleurothallidinae, a subtribe within the orchid family. This classification is based on its morphology, including its leaf and flower structure. Further research is needed to confirm the exact classification of Pabstiella tenera within the orchid family.

intro: Pabstiella tenera, commonly known as Pabst's Slender, is a species of orchid native to the tropical forests of Central and South America. The plant has been traditionally used in folk medicine to treat various health conditions, including fever, cough, and respiratory issues, due to its anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties.
